codeReview.summary: The commit keeps FinishedGoodsService as a facade and updates selected callers/tests, but the decomposition is not real: business logic was moved wholesale into a new 2,119-line FinishedGoodsWorkflowService while the four new focused services are pass-through delegators.

issues.description: Decomposition quality fails the feature intent and mission convention: a new god service (2,119 lines) centralizes nearly all original FinishedGoodsService logic instead of distributing real logic into catalog/stock/reservation/dispatch services.

FinishedGoodsCatalogService is only a thin forwarding layer to workflowService for all operations; no catalog-specific business logic was migrated into the focused service. The same pass-through pattern is repeated in FinishedGoodsStockService, FinishedGoodsReservationService, and FinishedGoodsDispatchService.
The refactor does not perform actual logic migration into the four focused services; it introduces a renamed monolith (FinishedGoodsWorkflowService) and keeps focused services as delegators.
issues.evidence: wc -l shows FinishedGoodsWorkflowService at 2119 lines and focused services at 38/58/22/64 lines; method bodies in FinishedGoodsCatalogService (lines 19-36), FinishedGoodsStockService (23-56), FinishedGoodsReservationService (15-20), and FinishedGoodsDispatchService (21-62) are direct workflowService forwards.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill enforces strict TDD ordering, but decomposition work in this mission is often executed as behavior-preserving refactor-first with immediate regression validation.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Handoff skillFeedback reports followedProcedure=false due to skipping strict Step 2 in favor of refactor-first. Codifying this allowed alternative for decomposition-heavy tasks would align procedure with real workflow and reduce avoidable process deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md (Step 2: Write Tests First) vs handoff skillFeedback.deviations in /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/9cf481ca-5e0f-423d-8241-8364a13a7137/handoffs/2026-02-28T16-41-42-891Z__decompose-finished-goods-service__f2a82b23-c599-404b-ae16-790dc07577d3.json

summary: Review failed: caller wiring and targeted tests were updated, but the core decomposition objective was not met because business logic remains concentrated in a new 2,119-line workflow service and the four focused services are pass-through wrappers rather than true domain-specific implementations.
